summary: PR #2608 matched and linked src/MSL/math.c by changing MSL/math.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, moving logf-related constants into math.c/.sdata2-style storage, removing coefficient definitions from math_data.c, and tightening logf expression/local structure. The PR body says the last bit required inlining after an unsuccessful Codex attempt. The bot report records 2 new matches: main/MSL/math .sdata2 to 100.00% and logf to 100.00%, with +364 matched/linked code bytes and +32 matched/linked data bytes.

key_files.role: Changed MSL/math.c object classification from NonMatching to Matching.

Main decomp/matching target; adjusted FloatU32 layout, added SECTION_SDATA2 constants, refactored logf locals/expressions, used __float_nan/__float_huge, and removed an old explanatory nonmatching assembly comment.
Removed __logf_C0_bits and __logf_C1_bits coefficient definitions from separate math data TU.

what_changed.text: configure.py now treats MSL/math.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
math.c adds SECTION_SDATA2 guarded by __MWERKS__ and !M2CTX, then defines MSL_Math_804DE1B0, MSL_Math_804DE1B4, __float_nan, and __float_huge in that section when applicable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: SECTION_SDATA2 and constants in src/MSL/math.c

what_changed.text: logf now loads polynomial coefficient bit patterns from MSL_Math_804DE1B0 and MSL_Math_804DE1B4 into a local u32 coef array instead of extern __logf_C0_bits/__logf_C1_bits from math_data.c.
logf inlines the polynomial expression into h2_poly and uses register float locals for H and h2_poly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: register float h2_poly/H and h2_poly expression
human_pr_text: 'it just required a bit of inlining'

what_changed.text: logf now uses repeated direct bit casts of x in switch/cases and casts E to float in LN2 multiplications.
The prior large commented assembly note about matching __one_over_F[m] base-pointer codegen was removed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: deleted comment block after logf

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded 2 new matches for main/MSL/math: .sdata2 and logf, and reported +364 matched/linked code bytes and +32 matched/linked data bytes.

smart_moves.text: Kept lookup tables __ln_F, __one_over_F, __sincos_on_quadrant, and __sincos_poly in math_data.c while moving only the logf coefficient bit patterns into math.c-local section-controlled storage.
Used a narrow configure.py status change only after the bot report showed math.c/logf matching and linked progress.
The final matching tweak was described by the author as inlining rather than relying on Codex output.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small MSL math mismatches, expression shape and inlining of intermediate polynomial computations can be decisive; here the author states the remaining bit required inlining, and the diff collapses C1/C0/new_var/poly intermediates into one h2_poly expression.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'Fortunately it just required a bit of inlining.'
raw_diff: h2_poly expression replaces multiple temporaries

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching math code that reinterprets float coefficients, the exact storage location and load path for coefficient bit patterns may matter; this PR moved logf coefficients from math_data.c externs to math.c SECTION_SDATA2 u32 globals and the bot report showed .sdata2 and logf matches.

naming_conventions.name: MSL_Math_804DE1B0
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR uses an address-style name for a recovered .sdata2 u32 constant rather than an inferred semantic coefficient name.

naming_conventions.name: MSL_Math_804DE1B4
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR uses an address-style name for the second recovered .sdata2 u32 constant.

naming_conventions.name: __float_nan / __float_huge
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR names special float bit-pattern constants with double-underscore MSL-style names and stores them as const FloatU32 in SECTION_SDATA2.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use compiler-section annotation behind an MWERKS/M2CTX guard for sdata2-sensitive constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is a tactic observed in this PR, not a proposed global rule; loaded standards require section/data ownership evidence and narrow verification for data-section edits.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use u32 bit-pattern globals plus local u32 coef array and float pointer reinterpretation to shape loads for logf coefficients.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The evidence supports this tactic for this logf match only.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Collapse polynomial temporaries into a single expression and mark key float locals register to achieve final match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded standards treat register/local steering as an exception requiring verification; this PR has a bot report but no detailed objdiff transcript in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote object from NonMatching to Matching only when report evidence shows the target unit/function reached 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists logf 100.00% and .sdata2 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice contains bot summary evidence, not full build logs.

follow_up_queries: PRs touching SECTION_SDATA2 or __declspec(section ".sdata2") in MSL files
PRs where register float locals were used to close a math function match
PRs moving constants between math_data.c and math.c
MSL logf coefficient symbol naming MSL_Math_804DE1B0 MSL_Math_804DE1B4
PRs mentioning Codex and final inlining tweaks

evidence_refs: raw_diff: full diff for 3 changed files
notes: The slice includes the complete diff and bot summary report, enough to identify changed files, matching outcome, and concrete code tactics.
There are no review comments or human reviewer feedback in the slice.
The bot report gives summary matching/linking percentages and new matches, but no full build log, objdiff output, or adjacent-regression transcript.
The PR body mentions Codex and inlining, but does not explain all intent behind data placement or symbol naming choices.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inlining expression shape closed MSL logf
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2608, the author said the last bit of matching math.c required inlining; the diff replaced several polynomial temporaries in logf with one h2_poly expression and the bot report recorded logf at 100.00%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: MSL logf coefficient placement affected match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2608 moved logf coefficient bit patterns out of math_data.c extern definitions and into math.c SECTION_SDATA2 u32 globals named MSL_Math_804DE1B0/MSL_Math_804DE1B4; the bot report recorded both main/MSL/math .sdata2 and logf as new 100% matches.

source_update_candidates.title: Document register/local steering evidence requirements with PR #2608 as an example
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2608 used register float locals and inlined expression shape in logf while matching math.c. If curated, it could be referenced as an example of narrow register/local-expression steering that should remain tied to matching evidence rather than treated as general style.
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards already say register/local steering needs evidence; this PR may provide a concrete example but should not broaden the rule.

source_update_candidates.title: MSL/math.c was linked as Matching in PR #2608
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2608 changed configure.py so MSL/math.c is Object(Matching, "MSL/math.c") instead of Object(NonMatching, "MSL/math.c").
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible historical path/status fact for curator verification against current source.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that Codex cannot solve similar matches; the body only reports this author's experience on this PR.
Do not promote SECTION_SDATA2 data movement as a default matching style; this PR supports it only for these MSL/math.c constants and has no full section-ownership analysis in the slice.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; the slice has no review comments or reviews.
Do not claim full regression coverage beyond the bot summary report included in text_corpus.
